entries.title: Patent application filed: evidence artifacts for unstructured audit requests
entries.blurb: Co-filed a USPTO application with a teammate at Vanta. The idea: turn unstructured audit requests into structured workflows by reasoning over a repository of evidence and signals from prior audits. Patent pending.

entries.title: Promoted to Senior Engineering Manager at Vanta
entries.blurb: Same teams, but the scope grew.

entries.title: Shipped the college football scorigami visualization
entries.blurb: A heatmap of every final score that has ever happened in college football. Wrote about it for the blog.

entries.title: Built this site in Astro
entries.blurb: Wanted something tiny, fast, and easy to build with.

entries.title: Joined Vanta as a Software Engineering Manager
entries.blurb: Came in to run the Partner Experiences team. Compliance is a complex problem.

entries.title: Joined Elation Health as a Software Engineering Manager
entries.blurb: Started mid-pivot toward value-based care as a company. Value-based care is still coming any day now.

entries.title: Moved to Columbus
entries.blurb: Closer to family. The remote-work era made it work.

entries.title: Joined Automox as a Software Engineering Manager
entries.blurb: Took over a brand-new applications pillar. Two engineers became thirteen, and the broader org went from sixty to two hundred.

entries.title: Completed the Purdue cybersecurity program
entries.blurb: Twelve weeks of threat modeling, incident response, and trying to break things.

entries.title: Tech Lead and Product Owner at Eli Lilly
entries.blurb: Android tech lead and product owner for Tempo, Lilly's Class II personalized diabetes management app.

entries.title: Joined Eli Lilly as a Tech Lead in Digital Health
entries.blurb: First full-time role with a degree. Tech lead on Think. Talk. Treat. Migraine — Lilly's Class I iOS app for improving migraine conversations between patients and their doctors.

entries.title: BS in Computer Science, Miami University
entries.blurb: Met many of my closest friends here. Was working as a SWE before crossing the stage.

entries.title: Became an ICAgile Certified Professional
entries.blurb: After enough retrospectives, the certification mostly confirmed what teams had already taught me.

entries.title: Founded Riley Media, LLC
entries.blurb: Client work alongside my own products. The biggest: a system that scraped breaking financial news, ran sentiment analysis on it, and drove trading decisions off the signal. Earned an offer from a top-ten US startup accelerator for another product.

entries.title: First SWE job at Dropoly
entries.blurb: Educational and audit apps in the energy sector. Ported an ActionScript codebase to run on Android, then helped rebuild the whole thing in Meteor.js after we pivoted.

entries.title: YouTube channel hit partnership status
entries.blurb: A run of game-dev tutorials. Apparently a lot of other people wanted to learn how to ship a game.

entries.title: First Android app crossed 100,000 downloads
entries.blurb: A lost-phone finder, built after watching a couple fight over a lost one at a party. Pre-dated Find My Phone — turned out other people had the same problem.

entries.title: Banned from eBay for selling game hacks
entries.blurb: Sold PlayStation and Call of Duty hacks until eBay caught on. Early entrepreneurial spirit, slightly misguided execution.

entries.title: Built my gaming clan a website
entries.blurb: First time someone asked me to build something for them. A crappy phpBB install on free hosting. Felt like magic anyway.
